value.reason: The cited page is explicitly titled "Prompt caching | OpenAI API" and is listed under the Context management section of the OpenAI API docs, confirming it documents prompt/context caching as a shipped feature for OpenAI — distinct from context compaction (listed separately), MCP, or persistent memory. The label "Prompt Caching" matches the page title exactly. The description's claims — automatic caching of the longest reusable prefix, cost and latency reduction for repeated context (system prompts, few-shot examples), discounted billing for cached tokens, and transparent application without code changes — are all consistent with OpenAI's documented prompt caching behavior. No invented claims are present.
